title: Implement Rust SDK core API: channels, conversations, and type enforcement
purpose: Deliver the core application-facing API surface of the Rust SDK: ChannelHandle for typed publish/subscribe/request-reply (ADR-002), ConversationHandle for supervised message exchange (ADR-001), and the SchemaValidate trait for compile-time type enforcement. These types define the contract every deployment mode (embedded, remote) must satisfy.
task: Create channel.rs, conversation.rs, and types.rs in crates/agora-sdk/src/. ChannelHandle is a trait with generic methods constrained by Serialize+SchemaValidate (publish) and Deserialize (subscribe). ConversationHandle is a trait with send/receive/lifecycle observation. SchemaValidate is a trait that message types implement to provide schema metadata for publish-time validation. All types are trait definitions at this stage -- concrete implementations come in SDK-004 (embedded/remote modes). IN SCOPE: trait definitions, type constraints, associated types, error integration with SdkError. OUT OF SCOPE: concrete implementations, connection management, backpressure types, embedded/remote distinction.

requirements.title: Define ChannelHandle trait with typed publish, subscribe, and request-reply
requirements.spec: WHEN a caller invokes publish on a ChannelHandle, THE SYSTEM SHALL require the message type to implement Serialize + SchemaValidate. WHEN a caller invokes subscribe, THE SYSTEM SHALL return a stream of messages whose type implements Deserialize. WHEN a caller invokes request_reply, THE SYSTEM SHALL send a message and return a future resolving to the reply. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT expose protocol framing or wire details through the ChannelHandle interface.
acceptance: ChannelHandle::publish<M: Serialize + SchemaValidate> compiles and accepts a typed message
ChannelHandle::subscribe returns an impl Stream<Item = Result<M, SdkError>> where M: Deserialize
ChannelHandle::request_reply<Req, Resp> sends Req and returns Future<Output = Result<Resp, SdkError>>
Attempting to publish a type not implementing SchemaValidate produces a compile-time error
ChannelHandle is object-safe or documents why it is not and provides an alternative

requirements.title: Define ConversationHandle trait with send, receive, and lifecycle observation
requirements.spec: WHEN a caller invokes send on a ConversationHandle, THE SYSTEM SHALL transmit a typed message within the conversation context. WHEN a caller invokes receive, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the next message in the conversation. THE SYSTEM SHALL provide lifecycle observation (opened, message, closing, closed, error) as a stream of ConversationEvent variants. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT require callers to manage conversation process identifiers directly.
acceptance: ConversationHandle::send<M: Serialize> accepts a typed message and returns Result<(), SdkError>
ConversationHandle::receive<M: Deserialize> returns Future<Output = Result<M, SdkError>>
ConversationHandle::lifecycle returns an impl Stream<Item = ConversationEvent>
ConversationEvent enum has variants: Opened, Message, Closing, Closed, Error
Each ConversationEvent variant carries a conversation identifier

requirements.title: Define SchemaValidate trait for compile-time type enforcement
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a SchemaValidate trait that message types implement to declare their schema. The trait SHALL provide a method returning schema metadata (name, version, and schema bytes). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT perform runtime schema validation in this trait -- it provides the metadata that the bus uses for publish-time validation per ADR-002.
acceptance: SchemaValidate trait is defined in types.rs with a schema_metadata() method
schema_metadata() returns a struct containing schema name, version, and schema bytes
The trait has a blanket or derive-macro path documented for user types
SchemaValidate is re-exported from the crate root

requirements.title: Enforce type parameter constraints on publish and subscribe
requirements.spec: WHEN a publish method is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL require the type parameter to satisfy both Serialize and SchemaValidate bounds. WHEN a subscribe method is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL require the type parameter to satisfy DeserializeOwned. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT allow untyped publish or subscribe operations.
acceptance: A struct implementing Serialize + SchemaValidate can be published without additional ceremony
A struct implementing only Serialize (not SchemaValidate) cannot be passed to publish -- compile error
Subscribe's type parameter requires DeserializeOwned, not Deserialize<'a> with a lifetime
No method on ChannelHandle accepts raw bytes or untyped payloads

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement concrete channel or conversation types -- only trait definitions.
SHALL NOT implement connection management or transport logic.
SHALL NOT implement backpressure response types (deferred to SDK-003).
SHALL NOT implement embedded or remote mode distinction (deferred to SDK-004).
SHALL NOT add network dependencies to the crate.
verification: cargo check -p agora-sdk compiles cleanly with trait definitions
cargo clippy -p agora-sdk -- -D warnings passes with no diagnostics
cargo fmt -p agora-sdk --check reports no formatting issues
All public traits and types have documentation comments
No file exceeds 500 lines of code
